human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for warranty-fulfillment boundary discipline, source quality, proof-of-purchase and serial handling, refund and policy-exception boundaries, safety-stop behavior, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to consumer-electronics and appliance warranty execution, coverage and service-order handling, completion evidence, exception progression, and safety-stop escalation.

Authority sources cover FTC warranty-law guidance and rules, current CPSC reporting and recall guidance, and current OEM public service and evidence workflows relevant to appliance and electronics warranty fulfillment.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete inputs, unsupported completion, booking or payment mismatch, safety escalation, refund-exception routing, rework, boundary rejection, high-volume queue handling, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation with fraud-oriented evidence controls.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-electronics-and-appliance-retail-and-service/warranty-fulfillment-specialist/ so warranty fulfillment stays separate from guest service, pricing, returns, fraud investigation, and operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around refund authority, pricing exceptions, recall handling, and unsupported completion.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated warranty fulfillment specialist that supports evidence-backed dealer warranty progression, service exception handling, and policy-safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
